text: Hab. 2.4. It is not certain whether the expression ‘faith first and last’ (literally, ‘from faith to faith’) implies a progress from belief in the Old Testament prophecies to belief in our Lord, or a progress from the act of faith by which we become Christians to the spirit of faith in which, as Christians, we ought to live.

vv. 18-27: Many of the considerations here brought forward are to be found in Wis., ch. 13. It is not clear, in verse 18, whether the heathen are blamed for ‘holding the truth and yet sinning’, or for ‘hindering the truth by means of their sin’.
The words ‘they never grasped the truth’ are not found in the best Greek manuscripts; these read, at the end of the verse, ‘they not only commit such acts, but countenance those who commit them’.
